departments.name: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
departments.description: The Maldivian government ministry responsible for climate policy, environmental protection, energy transition, waste management, and conservation of the country's marine and terrestrial ecosystems.

departments.name: Ministry of Health
departments.description: Responsible for healthcare delivery, disease prevention, public health programs, and medical regulation across all inhabited islands in the Maldives.

departments.name: Ministry of Education
departments.description: Oversees primary, secondary, and higher education policy, curriculum development, teacher training, and educational infrastructure throughout the country.

departments.name: Ministry of Transport and Civil Aviation
departments.description: Manages inter-island transportation, aviation policy, maritime safety, port development, and transportation infrastructure planning.

departments.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
departments.description: Handles diplomatic relations, international treaties, passport and visa services, and represents Maldivian interests in international forums.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Coral reef bleaching monitoring data 2023
requests.description: I request the ministry's coral reef bleaching monitoring data for the 2023 monitoring cycle, including site-level bleaching percentages and the survey methodology used.
requests.response: The ministry's 2023 coral reef monitoring summary has been published on the official portal. Site-level data and methodology are referenced in the linked monitoring report. Where individual site data is not yet released, please file a targeted follow-up request specifying the atoll(s) of interest.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Status of single-use plastic ban enforcement
requests.description: Please provide details of enforcement actions taken under the single-use plastic phase-out policy for the period January to December 2023, including the number of inspections, warnings issued, and any penalties imposed.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Renewable energy capacity installed in 2023
requests.description: I would like the breakdown of new renewable energy generation capacity (solar PV, wind, other) commissioned during calendar year 2023, including capacity in MW by island or atoll.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Coastal protection budget allocations 2024
requests.description: Please provide the budget allocations for coastal protection and shoreline reinforcement projects under the ministry for the 2024 fiscal year, broken down by island and project type.
requests.response: Aggregate budget figures for coastal protection are listed in the ministry's published budget statement. Project-level allocations are released as individual projects are tendered; please specify the island or project of interest in a follow-up request for further detail.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Environmental Impact Assessment reports for resort developments
requests.description: I request access to the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports approved by the ministry for new resort developments in Baa Atoll and Raa Atoll during 2022-23.
requests.response: Approved EIA reports are publicly available in the ministry's EIA repository. Specific reports for the atolls mentioned can be located by project name or developer; please refer to the public EIA listings or file a follow-up request with the specific project names if you require copies.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Waste management infrastructure status in northern atolls
requests.description: Kindly provide the operational status of regional waste management centres in the northern atolls as of Q1 2024, including any centres that are non-operational and the reasons for downtime.

requests.subject: COVID-19 vaccination statistics by atoll
requests.description: Please provide the COVID-19 vaccination coverage rates (first dose, second dose, booster) for all atolls as of December 2023, broken down by age group if available.
requests.response: National vaccination statistics including atoll-level coverage are published quarterly on the Health Protection Agency website. The Q4 2023 report includes the requested breakdown by atoll and age group. Age-specific data for smaller atolls may be aggregated to protect individual privacy.

requests.subject: Doctor-to-patient ratios in regional hospitals
requests.description: I request current doctor-to-patient ratios for all tertiary and regional hospitals, along with any WHO benchmarks the ministry uses for staffing adequacy assessments.
requests.response: Doctor-to-patient ratios vary by hospital type and specialty. The ministry compiles this data annually in the health workforce report. The 2023 report will be published by March 31, 2024. WHO benchmarks are considered alongside national service standards in workforce planning.

requests.subject: Maternal mortality rates 2020-2023
requests.description: Please provide maternal mortality statistics for the years 2020 through 2023, including causes where available, and any policy interventions implemented to address trends.
requests.response: Maternal mortality data is published in the annual health statistics report. The 2023 preliminary figures show continued progress toward national targets. Detailed cause-of-death analysis and policy response measures are outlined in the Safe Motherhood programme annual review available on the ministry portal.

requests.subject: Teacher vacancy rates in outer atolls
requests.description: I would like information on current teacher vacancy rates in schools located in atolls outside of Male' and Addu City, particularly for secondary science and mathematics positions.

requests.subject: School infrastructure upgrade budget 2024
requests.description: Please provide the capital budget allocated for school infrastructure upgrades and new classroom construction in 2024, broken down by atoll or region.
requests.response: The 2024 education sector capital budget is detailed in the ministry's budget publication. Infrastructure projects are prioritized based on student enrollment projections and facility condition assessments. Atoll-specific allocations are updated as projects move to tender stage.

requests.department: Ministry of Transport and Civil Aviation
requests.subject: Ferry service reliability data 2023
requests.description: I request data on the on-time performance and cancellation rates for the government-subsidized ferry services operating in Raa, Baa, and Lhaviyani atolls during 2023.

requests.department: Ministry of Transport and Civil Aviation
requests.subject: Airport development projects planned for 2024-2025
requests.description: Please provide a list of domestic airport development or expansion projects planned for the 2024-2025 period, including projected timelines and investment amounts.
requests.response: Airport infrastructure projects are outlined in the National Transport Strategy and the Public Sector Investment Programme. Specific timelines and budgets are released as projects reach the feasibility study or tendering stage. The ministry will publish a consolidated project list in Q2 2024.

requests.subject: Bilateral agreements signed in 2023
requests.description: I request a list of all bilateral agreements and memoranda of understanding signed by the Maldives with foreign governments during calendar year 2023, including the subject matter of each agreement.
requests.response: Bilateral agreements are published in the official gazette and listed on the ministry's treaty database. The 2023 annual report includes a comprehensive list of agreements signed during the year, organized by country and subject area. Agreements subject to parliamentary ratification are also recorded in Majlis proceedings.

requests.subject: Passport processing times and backlog statistics
requests.description: Please provide current passport processing times for standard and expedited applications, as well as the size of any application backlog as of March 2024.
requests.response: Standard passport applications are currently processed within 10-14 working days; expedited applications within 3 working days. There is no significant backlog as of March 2024. Processing times are updated weekly on the immigration service website and may vary during peak travel seasons.

requests.subject: Mental health service availability in regional hospitals
requests.description: I request information on mental health services currently available at regional hospitals, including the number of psychiatrists, clinical psychologists, and counselors stationed outside of Male' area.

requests.subject: Higher education scholarship statistics 2023
requests.description: Please provide statistics on government-funded higher education scholarships awarded in 2023, including the number of scholarships by field of study and destination country.
requests.response: The ministry publishes an annual higher education scholarship report detailing the number of awards by programme, field, and country. The 2023 report is currently under internal review and will be published on the ministry portal by the end of March 2024.

requests.department: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
requests.subject: Marine protected area enforcement reports
requests.description: I request any enforcement or violation reports related to marine protected areas in 2023, including the number of incidents, types of violations, and penalties imposed.

faqs.question: What is the Right to Information (RTI) Act in the Maldives?
faqs.answer: The Maldives Right to Information Act (Act No. 1/2014) gives every citizen the right to request information held by public authorities. The Act establishes the duty of state institutions to disclose information proactively and respond to specific requests within statutory timelines.

faqs.question: Who can file an RTI request with the ministry?
faqs.answer: Any citizen of the Maldives may file a Right to Information request. You are not required to state the reason for your request, and the ministry must process your application in accordance with the Right to Information Act.

faqs.question: How long does the ministry have to respond?
faqs.answer: Under the Right to Information Act, public authorities are required to respond within the timelines prescribed by law. If the information concerns the life or liberty of a person, an expedited response is required.

faqs.question: What information can be withheld?
faqs.answer: Certain categories of information are exempt from disclosure under the Right to Information Act, including matters of national security, ongoing investigations, and personal information whose release would be an unwarranted invasion of privacy. Exemptions are not absolute and may be overridden by overriding public interest.

faqs.question: What can I do if my request is rejected or unanswered?
faqs.answer: If your request is rejected or you do not receive a response within the statutory period, you may file an appeal with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICOM) in accordance with the procedures set out in the Right to Information Act.

faqs.question: What kinds of information does the ministry publish proactively?
faqs.answer: The ministry publishes a range of information on its official portal, including environmental policy documents, approved Environmental Impact Assessments, energy statistics, climate adaptation programmes, and conservation initiatives. Where information you seek is not yet published, you may file a formal RTI request.

faqs.question: How should I phrase my RTI request to get a useful response?
faqs.answer: Be specific. Identify the data, document, or decision you are asking about, and include the relevant time period and geographic scope (island, atoll, project name). Vague or overly broad requests are slower to process and may need clarification.

faqs.question: Can I request information from multiple departments at once?
faqs.answer: You should file separate RTI requests for each ministry or department, as different authorities maintain different records and have separate information officers responsible for processing requests.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for filing an RTI request?
faqs.answer: The Right to Information Act allows for nominal fees to cover the actual cost of reproduction and delivery of documents. However, fees cannot be used as a barrier to access. The first copy of any document is typically provided free of charge.

faqs.question: What happens if the information I requested doesn't exist?
faqs.answer: If the requested information does not exist or is not held by the authority, you will receive a written explanation. The authority is not required to create new information or conduct research to answer your request, but must disclose any existing records that are responsive to your query.
